div.h1: copy_from_user / copy_to_user: SMAP Sequence and Address Validation
div.h3: Step-by-step algorithm — x86-64 Linux, SMAP-enabled CPU

div.p: Warning: copy_from_user does not return a standard 0/-ERR negative code.
When a #PF occurs in kernel mode:

ol.li: OS searches .ex_table for the faulting RIP.

Example Entry:
fault_addr: 0xffffffff81001234 (The MOV instruction)
fixup_addr: 0xffffffff8100abcd (The Recovery code)
The CPU's Instruction Pointer is moved to fixup_addr.
Fixup code sets the return register to the remaining byte count and jumps to the end of the function.

ul.li: Rejects NULL/low-mem if SMAP/PAN enabled Cost: O(1) integer arithmetic.

div.p: Set Alignment Check (AC) flag in RFLAGS. SMAP (Supervisor Mode Access Prevention) is disabled for current CPU core. Ring 0 can now read userspace.
Core Loop: rep movsq or optimized MOV sequence. Reads from [src_user] -> Writes to [dst_kernel]. Each instruction address is registered in __ex_table.

ol.li: Match found? Redirect RIP to .fixup section.
Fixup code calculates residual = n - copied.

div.p: Clear AC flag in RFLAGS. SMAP is re-enabled. Ring 0 will now #GP fault if it touches userspace pointers outside this narrow window.

g.text: Ensures destination is in the user-permitted memory range.
Copies from Kernel Buffer to User Buffer.
